journals.name: Elsevier (Computers & Structures style)
style_notes: Expect strong computational or methodological novelty.
Require rigorous validation, benchmark comparisons, and clear numerical formulation.
Application-only studies require unusually strong evidence and generalizable insight.

journals.name: IEEE
style_notes: Emphasize technical rigor, clear system design, and reproducible evaluation.
Require fair baseline comparison when performance or efficiency claims are made.
Novelty may be architectural, empirical, or application-driven if clearly validated.

journals.name: MDPI
style_notes: Prioritize clarity, completeness, and sufficient empirical support.
Moderate novelty may be acceptable if the paper is coherent and useful.
Claims should be bounded to the evidence actually presented.

journals.name: MDPI Signals
style_notes: Emphasize signal-processing, sensing, measurement, and deployable system relevance.
Validation should be sufficient for the strength of application claims.
Avoid overstating generalization from limited real-world tests.

journals.name: Springer Nature — Engineering with Computers
style_notes: Emphasize computational method, numerical validation, reproducibility, and engineering relevance.
Require clear mathematical formulation and algorithm specification.
Require benchmark comparison against accepted methods.
Distinguish method contribution from case study.

journals.name: Springer Nature — Discover Mechanical Engineering
style_notes: Emphasize engineering applicability and practical relevance.
Require clear description of mechanical models, assumptions, and boundary conditions.
Encourage validation against experiments, simulations, or established engineering benchmarks.
Moderate novelty requirement: strong applications and validated workflows are acceptable.
Ensure clarity in methodology and reproducibility of engineering analysis.

journals.name: CAMES — Computer Assisted Methods in Engineering and Science
style_notes: Focus on computational methods, numerical modeling, and engineering science.
Require clear mathematical formulation, governing equations, and algorithm description when central to the paper.
Expect validation against analytical solutions, benchmarks, experiments, or high-fidelity simulations where applicable.
Emphasize mesh convergence, parameter sensitivity, and numerical stability when relevant.
Moderate novelty requirement: application-oriented computational studies may be acceptable if rigorously validated.
Clearly distinguish between numerical method contribution and engineering application.
